{{Quote|So, it seems the rat has run out of places to hide.|Mara Jade upon cornering Abron Mar|<ref name="Sith" />}}
'''Abron Mar''' was an [[Abron Mar's species|alien]] lieutenant in [[Takara]]'s criminal organization. He was known to frequent a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] located in the [[Katraasii]] [[Katraasii Spaceport|Spaceport]]. During the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] was tasked by [[Ka'Pa]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] to find Mar in order re-obtain a stolen [[Global Communications Transponder]] device from Takara. Jade tracked Mar to the spaceport, and after cutting off his escape routes, she cornered him. However, Mar had actually laid a trap and rendered Jade unconscious. He then sent her to [[Takara's stronghold]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Is this the best that Ka'Pa has to offer? Let's find our rat some suitable accommodations. I'm sure Takara will have some questions he'll want to ask her.|Abron Mar|<ref name="Sith" />}}
[[File:Abron_Mar_and_goons.png|thumb|left|200px|Abron Mar, after capturing Mara Jade]]
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of a [[Color/Legends|green]]-skinned [[Abron Mar's species|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]], Abron Mar served as a lieutenant for the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Takara]]. Mar was known to frequent a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] located in the [[Katraasii Spaceport]], on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Katraasii]].<ref name="Sith" /> During the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]],<ref name="TNEC">According to the opening scrawl, ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Mysteries of the Sith]]'' takes place five [[Standard year/Legends|standard years]] after the events of ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]''. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of ''Jedi Knight'' to [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. Using simple math, we can deduce that ''Mysteries of the Sith'' takes place in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]].</ref> Takara stole a [[Global Communications Transponder]] from his [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] rival, [[Ka'Pa]]. Around the same time, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], in need of supplies, sent the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] to [[Mission to negotiate supplies from Ka'Pa the Hutt|negotiate a deal]] with Ka'Pa to obtain said supplies. Ka'Pa saw this as a chance to retrieve his stolen property, and tasked Jade in retrieving the GCT device from Takara. The Hutt's agents informed Jade of Mar, and that he would be the key in reaching [[Takara's stronghold]]. By the time Jade made it to Katraasii, Mar was present in his favored [[Unidentified Katraasii cantina|cantina]], surrounded by [[Gran/Legends|Gran]] and [[Weequay/Legends|Weequay]] [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]]. Jade, not wanting to provoke Mar into running off, eventually found the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] where Mar had parked his personal [[R-41 Starchaser]] and sealed the hangar door.<ref name="Sith" />

Jade went back to the cantina to apprehend Mar, but the lieutenant escaped the cantina while Jade dealt with his cronies. With his [[Abron Mar's R-41 Starchaser|ship]] currently unable to take off due to the hangar having been sealed, Mar opted to run further into the spaceport with two Weequay and lay a trap for Jade. The Jedi fought her way through Mar's men and various [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[field stormtrooper]]s, and caught a glimpse of Mar and his Weequay heading through a small [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]] bay. Mar shut the bay doors on Jade, but the Jedi used a [[T-4 (vehicle)|T-4 troop transport]] to smash through the doors.<ref name="Sith" />

She continued to follow Mar until she finally cornered him in a storage room. Mar pretended to surrender, allowing Jade to let her guard down and get subsequently hit with a [[Stun/Legends|stun]] blast from behind. Mar then sent her to a prison in Takara's stronghold. Jade, however, eventually recovered the GCT device anyways.<ref name="Sith" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Who's the rat now? You walked right into my trap.|Abron Mar, after subduing Mara Jade|<ref name="Sith" />}}
Abron Mar enjoyed frequenting a cantina in the Katraasii spaceport. Upon capturing Mara Jade, Mar expressed disappointment in Jade's abilities, and was unimpressed with Ka'Pa's choice of agent. Mar was a member of a species that had green colored skin and three ridges in place of a [[Nose/Legends|nose]].<ref name="Sith" />

==Equipment==
Mar was armed with an [[E-11 blaster rifle/Legends|E-11 blaster rifle]] during the chase with Jade, and had his own personal R-41 Starchaser. Mar wore dark [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] with red stripes at the time, and wore goggles that concealed his [[Eye/Legends|eyes]].<ref name="Sith" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Mara then heads off to the spaceport city of Katraasii to hunt a guy named Abron Mar.|[[Ryan Kaufman (real person)|Ryan Kaufman]], the [[LucasArts]] continuity supervisor and level designer for ''Mysteries of the Sith'', speaks of Abron Mar|<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|RyanKaufman|4|Kyle and Mara: Making Mysteries of the Sith - Part II: Zombies of the Dark Side}}</ref>}}
Abron Mar first appeared in the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Mysteries of the Sith]]'', which was an expansion pack for the previously released ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]''. When Mara Jade moves within close enough to Abron Mar, a cutscene triggers in which she is captured.

It is technically possible to kill Abron by sniping him with the [[Blaster_rifle/Legends|Stormtrooper rifle]] equipped with the [[E-11 electroscope]], before the cutscene can take place, a single shot will make Abron Mar collapse to the ground, but the player will then get the message that completing the mission is now impossible with Abron Mar dead.

Mar's voice was provided by [[Terence McGovern]], and appears throughout two levels of the game. Mar's character model was also available as a multiplayer avatar in the game. In multiplayer, ''Mysteries of the Sith'' introduced multiplayer personalities, where character models were categorized by profession. Mar was categorized as a [[Scout/Legends|scout]].<ref name="Sith" />
